title: calawaya embreei
intro: Calawaya embreei, also known as the 'Andean mint', is a plant native to the Andean region of South America. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, headaches, and digestive issues, due to its high content of camphor and menthol. The health benefits of Calawaya embreei include its ability to reduce inflammation, relieve pain, and act as a natural antiseptic, making it a popular herbal remedy among locals. Calawaya embreei is often prepared as a tea by infusing its leaves in hot water, or as an essential oil through steam distillation, and is used to treat a variety of ailments, including colds, sore throats, and skin conditions.
